---

_**Agent (copilot/auto)**_

The error occurs because PowerShell's execution policy is blocking unsigned scripts, including npm.ps1. To resolve this, you can temporarily change the execution policy for the current session.

Run this command in your PowerShell terminal before running npm commands:

Set-ExecutionPolicy -Scope Process -ExecutionPolicy Bypass

This will allow unsigned scripts to run for the current session only. After running this, try your npm audit fix command again.

_**User (2026-03-16 14:33:12Z)**_

can I have it executed before upening any vscode terminal?

---

_**Agent (copilot/auto)**_

You cannot set PowerShell's execution policy for all VS Code terminals by default from within VS Code settings alone. However, you can make every new PowerShell terminal session start with the desired policy by adding the following line to your PowerShell profile script:

Set-ExecutionPolicy -Scope Process -ExecutionPolicy Bypass

To do this:

1. Open PowerShell and run:
notepad $PROFILE

2. Add this line at the end:
Set-ExecutionPolicy -Scope Process -ExecutionPolicy Bypass

3. Save and close Notepad.

Now, every new PowerShell terminal (including those opened by VS Code) will have the execution policy set to Bypass for that session.

---
